save_persistence_audit.summary: 9 findings (2 HIGH, 4 MEDIUM, 1 LOW, 4 PASS). Implemented SAVE-FINS-VUES-CLAMP + SAVE-TRUST-CLAMP in same commit.

findings.description: trust_merlin validated as numeric (line 276) but NOT clamped to 0-100 range after load. A tampered save with trust_merlin=999 would pass validation and immediately give T3 content (bible: T3 requires trust_merlin>80) from the first run. Fixed by adding clampi(trust_merlin, 0, 100) in load_profile() clamping block.

fins_vues and total_runs validated as numeric (lines 279-281) but NOT clamped to >=0 after load. Negative values would silently break arc conditions (fins_vues >= 5 check, biome maturity score = total_runs*2). Task SAVE-FINS-VUES-CLAMP (P31) resolved.

_validate() checks oghams has 'owned' and 'equipped' keys (line 266-268) but never verifies that equipped value is actually in the owned array. A tampered save with equipped='muin' (not in owned) would pass validation and the ogham system would start with an invalid active ogham, potentially causing null access in ogham activation.
echo_memory presence validated (is Dictionary) but sub-keys (deaths_by_biome, dominant_factions_seen, choices_log) not validated. Task SAVE-ECHO-MEMORY-SUBKEYS (P32) already queued.

biome_runs exists check present but sub-keys not validated. Empty biome_runs{} passes validation and silently gives 0 for all biome runs, breaking maturity score. Task SAVE-BIOME-RUNS-SUBKEYS (P34) already queued.

apply_run_rewards() (store_run.gd:392-412) increments total_runs, stats, biome_runs but NEVER appends to run_history[]. HubAntre.gd:887 reads run_history for UI display — always empty. Task SAVE-RUN-HISTORY-APPEND (P20) already queued.

load_run_state() clamps life_essence (line ~218) but does NOT clamp faction_rep_delta values. Bible s.3.3: caps ±20/carte. Tampered run_state could inject faction_rep_delta: {druides: 9999}, bypassing the per-card cap of ±20.

stats Dictionary validated to exist (line 248) but sub-keys not validated as non-negative integers. stats.total_deaths=-5 would cause consecutive_deaths logic errors. stats.total_cards=-1 would break maturity score.
Backup strategy (backup before write) is correct. save_profile() calls _backup_file() before _write_file(), ensuring backup has last valid state on crash.
Legacy migration (_try_migrate_from_legacy) handles slot1-3 + autosave, wraps in profile format, cleans up legacy files. Pattern correct.
faction_rep properly clamped 0-100 for all 5 factions after load. Pattern consistent.
_validate_run_state() validates all required keys including anam_accumulated (added by SPEC-9). life_max >= 1 check prevents division by zero.

llm_test_1_execution.summary: 5 invariants checked (fallback, poll_result, cancel, double-call, busy-leak). 1 WARN_LOW race condition. 4 PASS. No CRITICAL bugs.

invariants.invariant: Fallback card used when Ollama times out
invariants.detail: _run_llm() returns {error: 'LLM timeout (Nms)'} on timeout (merlin_ai.gd:295). generate_narrative/structured propagate error dict. merlin_game_controller.gd:414-415 checks dispatch failure and uses FastRoute fallback immediately. Chain is intact.

invariants.invariant: poll_result() correct — calls callback exactly once, clears state
invariants.detail: poll_result() (ollama_backend.gd:166-195) correctly checks _result_ready under mutex, calls callback, clears _pending_callback, waits for thread. BUT cancel_generation() does NOT clear _pending_callback. After cancel, if thread writes _result_ready=true (race window ~ms), a subsequent poll_result() call delivers stale result via stale callback. Risk: LOW — generate_async() resets _result_ready before new call; only an external poll_result() between cancel and next generate would trigger stale delivery. Recommend: add _pending_callback = Callable() to cancel_generation().

cancel_generation() sets _cancel_requested=true (checked by _blocking_generate to abort HTTP), _is_generating=false, _stream_done=true. Thread continues but _blocking_generate exits early. generate_async() calls _thread.wait_to_finish() before new generation, ensuring clean handoff.

invariants.invariant: generate_async() errors on double-call (no concurrent generations)
invariants.detail: ollama_backend.gd:141: if _is_generating: callback({error: 'Already generating'}); return. Double-call correctly returns error via callback without spawning second thread.

invariants.invariant: Brain busy flags (_primary_narrator_busy, _primary_gm_busy) cleared on LLM timeout
invariants.detail: generate_narrative() sets _primary_narrator_busy=true, awaits _run_llm() (which handles timeout internally and returns error dict), then sets _primary_narrator_busy=false regardless of error/success (lines 1562-1563). Same pattern for generate_structured (lines 1591-1592). No busy flag leak on timeout. 60s watchdog in _process() (line 2066) provides secondary protection.

new_bug_tasks.title: cancel_generation() does not clear _pending_callback — stale callback race
new_bug_tasks.description: ollama_backend.gd:200-211: cancel_generation() sets _cancel_requested=true but does not clear _pending_callback=Callable(). If _blocking_generate() completes (writes _result_ready=true) after cancel returns but before next generate_async() call, a stray poll_result() would invoke the stale callback with the old result. Fix: add _pending_callback = Callable() inside cancel_generation() before _mutex.lock().
